items.final_wording: Supports clinician-performed general inspection, mouth-breathing observation, airflow assessment, anterior rhinoscopy, secretions, septal appearance, polyps, crusting, throat, and postnasal-drip documentation.

source.title: BSACI guideline for the diagnosis and management of allergic and non-allergic rhinitis (Revised Edition 2017; First edition 2007)
source.jurisdiction: United Kingdom specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE allergy, ENT, prescribing, and referral adaptation
source.population: Children and adults with suspected or established allergic, non-allergic, occupational, or other rhinitis phenotypes, subject to section-specific qualifiers.
source.setting: Primary, allergy, respiratory, paediatric, and ENT assessment and review.
source.exclusions: Exact for rhinitis history, triggers, nasal and eye symptoms, sleep and mouth-breathing impact, medicine and social context, nasal examination, septum, secretions, crusting, polyps, postnasal drip, and clinician-selected testing context. It does not diagnose a subtype or generate treatment.

exact_location.heading: Sections 8.1–8.3 — visual, nasal, oral, and upper-airway examination

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-performed general inspection, mouth-breathing observation, airflow assessment, anterior rhinoscopy, secretions, septal appearance, polyps, crusting, throat, and postnasal-drip documentation.

items.rationale: The exact BSACI examination section supports clinician-performed general and ENT examination without auto-populating nasal findings.
